title: Structured Parameterization and Non-Stabilizerness in Hypergraph QAOA
authors: Evan Camilleri, André Xuereb, Tony J. G. Apollaro, Mirko Consiglio
abstract: The quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA) has emerged as a promising candidate for demonstrating quantum advantage on noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices. While various QAOA parameterization schemes exist, ranging from the original single-angle approach to the more expressive multi-angle quantum approximate optimization algorithm (MA-QAOA) and automorphic-angle quantum approximate optimization algorithm (AA-QAOA), each presents distinct trade-offs between expressiveness and classical optimization complexity. In this work, we introduce the $k$-interaction-angle quantum approximate optimization algorithm ($k$A-QAOA), a parameterization scheme that groups cost function terms by their $k$-body interaction order, providing a natural middle ground between parameter efficiency and solution quality. This approach is particularly well-suited for combinatorial optimization problems defined on hypergraphs, where multi-body interactions naturally arise in applications such as Boolean satisfiability and resource allocation with multi-party constraints. We benchmark $k$A-QAOA against standard single-angle quantum approximate optimization algorithm (SA-QAOA), MA-QAOA, and AA-QAOA on two problem classes: 3-uniform cyclic sign-alternating hypergraphs and random coefficient hypergraphs. Our results demonstrate that $k$A-QAOA achieves approximation ratios comparable to MA-QAOA while requiring significantly fewer function evaluations, thereby reducing quantum resource consumption.
summary: This paper introduces kA-QAOA, a new parameterization for the QAOA algorithm that groups interactions by their order. It demonstrates that this method can match the performance of more complex multi-angle approaches while being much more efficient to optimize, making it highly suitable for NISQ-era hardware with multi-qubit gates.
main_problem: Finding an optimal QAOA parameterization that balances solution quality (expressiveness) with classical optimization complexity (number of parameters and function evaluations) for hypergraph-based optimization problems.
main_result: The proposed kA-QAOA achieves approximation ratios comparable to the highly expressive MA-QAOA but requires significantly fewer function evaluations and parameters, effectively acting as a middle ground between efficiency and quality.
method: The authors introduce kA-QAOA, which groups cost function terms by their k-body interaction order, and benchmark it against SA, MA, and AA-QAOA using classical optimizers (BFGS, Powell) and TQA initialization.
model_or_system: The study focuses on the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) applied to combinatorial optimization problems on hypergraphs, specifically 3-uniform cyclic sign-alternating hypergraphs and random coefficient hypergraphs using k-body Ising-like Hamiltonians.
key_observables: Approximation Ratio (AR), Fidelity, Number of Function Evaluations (nfev), and Non-stabilizerness (Stabilizer Renyi Entropy).
important_parameters: Number of QAOA layers (p), interaction order (k), and parameter initialization strategies (e.g., 1/p, TQA0.75).
main_assumptions: The approach assumes the availability of hardware capable of executing native multi-qubit gates (e.g., trapped ions or neutral atoms) to avoid the overhead of decomposing k-body terms into two-qubit gates.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a 4-qubit cost Hamiltonian circuit; Figure 3 compares performance metrics (magic, AR, nfev) across QAOA variants; Figures 4-7 show AR, fidelity, and nfev for specific hypergraph problem classes across different layers.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the kA-QAOA scheme, defines the hypergraph problem classes and Hamiltonians, details the benchmarking methodology and optimization strategies, presents comparative results for different hypergraph types, and discusses the implications regarding magic and resource efficiency.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: kA-QAOA four-qubit quantum circuit for Eq. (5) with p = 1 layers having parameterized angles γ1 for Rzi (blue) gates, γ2 for Rzizj (green) gates, γ3 for Rzizjzk (yellow) gates, as well as β1, β2, β3, and β4 for Rxi (red) gates.

Figure 2: 5-vertex 3-uniform cyclic hypergraph representation. Each node is represented by a yellow circle with a number i inside it. The hyperedges are represented by areas, denoted by ei, which encapsulate the nodes they link. For example, hyperedge e1 links nodes 1, 2, and 3.

Figure 3: Comparison of QAOA performance metrics: (a) average normalized magic, (b) average AR, and (c) average nfev for the POWELL optimizer with TQA parameter initialization, all shown with standard deviation.
Figure 4: Approximation Ratio for 3-uniform cyclic sign-alternating hypergraphs with p ∈{1, 2, 3} for the cost function C(x) = Pn−k i=0 (−1)i Qk−1 j=0 xi+j where n ∈[4, 15], k = 3.
Figure 5: Fidelity for 3-uniform cyclic sign-alternating hypergraphs with p ∈{1, 2, 3} for the cost function C(x) = Pn−k i=0 (−1)i Qk−1 j=0 xi+j where n ∈[4, 15], k = 3.
